Code fragment: public static final BiFunction<Field<String>, String, Condition> STR_LIKE = (f, v) -> f.like(likeWrap(v));
Explanation: The STR_LIKE function wraps user input with '%' wildcards (via likeWrap) to perform full-text search using LIKE, which is inefficient for large datasets and doesn't support relevance ranking. Consider using a dedicated full-text search solution like PostgreSQL's tsvector/tsquery or Elasticsearch.

Code fragment: public static final BiFunction<Field<String>, String, Condition> STR_LIKE_IC = (f, v) -> f.likeIgnoreCase(likeWrap(v));
Explanation: The STR_LIKE_IC function uses likeIgnoreCase with '%' wildcards (via likeWrap) for case-insensitive full-text search, which cannot use indexes efficiently and scales poorly. Consider using a proper full-text search engine or database-native full-text search capabilities.

Code fragment: public final TableField<CartsRecord, UUID> ID = createField(DSL.name("id"), SQLDataType.UUID.nullable(false).defaultValue(DSL.field(DSL.raw("uuid_generate_v4()"), SQLDataType.UUID)), this, "");
Explanation: The primary key column is generically named 'id' instead of a more descriptive name like 'cart_id'. Consider renaming the column to 'cart_id' to make foreign key references self-documenting and avoid ambiguity in queries with multiple joined tables.

Code fragment: public final TableField<CartsRecord, CartStatus> STATUS = createField(DSL.name("status"), SQLDataType.VARCHAR.nullable(false).asEnumDataType(CartStatus.class), this, "");
Explanation: The 'status' column uses an ENUM type (CartStatus) to restrict allowed values, which makes adding or removing status values difficult without schema changes. Consider creating a separate 'cart_statuses' lookup table with a foreign key reference to allow easier maintenance of status values.
